scoring_description: Each option scored independently as P(argument_text | 'Topic: {topic}. The strongest argument is: ') using average token logprob

problems.description: 46% of tasks have top-2 quality gap < 0.10 on a 0-1 scale. 26% have gap < 0.05. The 'correct' answer is essentially ambiguous in nearly half the tasks.
problems.cause: Dataset is 758 strong / 203 medium / 39 weak. Task generator fills 4 slots from the largest tier (strong), producing tasks where all options have similar quality.

problems.description: Sequence logprob scoring measures text fluency/probability, not argument quality assessment. Each option is scored independently as a continuation, so the model never sees all 4 options together to compare them.
All McNemar pairwise p-values > 0.24. No pair of models is statistically distinguishable. All CIs overlap completely.
All models show preference for option A (28-36% of predictions vs 26.5% true base rate).
problems.cause: Sequence scoring with shared prefix may create systematic bias toward the first option evaluated.

fixes_planned: Option 1: Enforce min_quality_gap >= 0.15 in task generation + require multi-tier sampling
Option 2: Switch to single-token MCQ scoring (present full prompt with all 4 options, extract logprobs for A/B/C/D label tokens)
Option 3 (if needed): v2.5 pairwise comparison benchmark
